label: Peak Evacuees (2011)
source: Cabinet Office of Japan / Reconstruction Agency
deltaNote: Primarily elderly; return rates low across most municipalities

label: Current Restricted Area
source: Fukushima Prefectural Government / Japan NRA (as of Dec 2025)
delta: ↓ from ~1,150 km² in 2012; reduced from ~600 km² original 20 km zone
deltaNote: Difficult-to-return zones in Okuma, Futaba still closed; represents ~2.2% of Fukushima Prefecture

label: Decommissioning Target
contestNote: Fuel debris retrieval has not meaningfully begun as of 2025; most independent experts consider 2051 completion highly optimistic

label: ALPS-Treated Water Discharged
contestNote: China lifted its near-total ban June 30, 2025 (partial restrictions on 10 prefectures including Fukushima remain); JFUA fisheries union continues to oppose; Hong Kong/Macau retain partial restrictions

label: Thyroid Cancer Cases (Fukushima Cohort)
contestNote: UNSCEAR and most epidemiologists attribute cases predominantly to ultrasensitive ultrasound screening effect; minority of studies find statistically significant radiation association

label: Est. Total Decommissioning Cost
source: METI Nuclear Damage Liability Facilitation Fund (2016 revised estimate)
contestNote: Some independent analyses estimate total societal costs including compensation, reconstruction, and foregone energy at ¥50–70 trillion ($450–630B)

label: Estimated Melted Fuel Debris (Units 1–3)
source: TEPCO / International Research Institute for Nuclear Decommissioning (IRID)
contestNote: Actual location and physical state of corium in three reactor vessels is largely unconfirmed; estimates based on reactor physics models

label: Disaster-Related Deaths (Fukushima Pref.)
deltaNote: Deaths caused by evacuation stress, inadequate medical care, suicide; exceeds direct tsunami/earthquake deaths in Fukushima. No direct radiation deaths confirmed.
